stem: The area of a triangle is 63 square centimeters. The length of the base of the triangle is 11 centimeters greater than the height of the triangle. What is the height, in centimeters, of the triangle?
rationale: Let h be the height. Then the base is h + 11, and the area is (1/2) · h · (h + 11) = 63. So h(h + 11) = 126, giving h² + 11h - 126 = 0. Factoring: (h + 18)(h - 7) = 0. Since h must be positive, h = 7. Choice D is correct. Choices A, B, and C arise from arithmetic or set-up errors (such as confusing base and height or forgetting the factor of 1/2).
